instruction: Read each passage carefully and answer the questions that follow. Answers should be based only on the given paragraph.

passages.paragraph: Vikram Seth describes his visit to Kathmandu in his travelogue. After reaching the city, he finds a cheap room in the center of Kathmandu and sleeps for several hours. The next morning, he goes with Mr Shah’s son and nephew to visit two famous temples that are important for Hindus and Buddhists.
questions: Who wrote the lesson Kathmandu?
Where did the narrator find a cheap room?
What did the narrator do after reaching Kathmandu?
Who accompanied the narrator to the temples?
Which two religions are associated with the temples?

passages.paragraph: At the Pashupatinath temple there is an atmosphere of confusion and noise. Priests, devotees, tourists, animals and birds move around everywhere. People push forward to get a good view of the deity and offer prayers.
questions: Which temple did the narrator visit first?
How is the atmosphere at the temple described?
Who are present in the temple area?
What do the devotees try to do?
Why is the temple crowded?

passages.paragraph: While the narrator is observing the temple, a princess of the Nepalese royal family arrives. Everyone quickly bows and makes way for her. At the same time, some Western tourists try to enter the temple but they are stopped by the police because only Hindus are allowed inside.
questions: Who arrives at the temple during the narrator’s visit?
How do people react to the princess?
Who tries to enter the temple after that?
Why are the tourists stopped by the police?
Who is allowed to enter the temple?

passages.paragraph: Suddenly two monkeys begin fighting and chasing each other across the temple courtyard. One monkey jumps onto a sacred shivalinga and runs towards the river. Near the Bagmati river, people are performing cremation rituals.
questions: What unexpected event occurs in the temple courtyard?
Where does one monkey jump during the chase?
Towards which river does the monkey run?
What rituals are taking place near the river?
What does this scene show about temple life?

passages.paragraph: On the bank of the Bagmati river, a small shrine stands partially submerged in water. People believe that when the shrine emerges completely from the river, the goddess inside will escape and the present evil age of Kaliyug will end.
questions: Where is the small shrine located?
What is special about the shrine?
What belief do people have about the shrine?
What will happen when the shrine emerges fully?
Which age is believed to end at that time?

passages.paragraph: After visiting the temple, the narrator goes to the Boudhanath stupa, an important Buddhist shrine in Kathmandu. Unlike the crowded temple, the stupa has a peaceful and calm environment. The large white dome stands in the center with shops around it.
questions: Which place does the narrator visit next?
Which religion is associated with the stupa?
How is the atmosphere at Boudhanath different from the temple?
What stands in the center of the stupa?
What is located around the stupa?

passages.paragraph: Kathmandu is a lively city with narrow streets filled with vendors and tourists. There are fruit sellers, postcard sellers and flute sellers. Shops sell many items such as cosmetics, chocolates and Nepalese antiques.
questions: How are the streets of Kathmandu described?
Who are some of the vendors mentioned?
What items are sold in the shops?
Why is the city described as lively?
What type of atmosphere exists in the streets?

passages.paragraph: The narrator enjoys the market and buys roasted corn with salt and chilli. He also drinks Coca-Cola and an orange drink. As he walks through the busy streets, he begins thinking about how he will return home.
questions: What food does the narrator buy in the market?
What drinks does he have?
Where does he buy these items?
What does he start thinking about afterwards?
What does this show about his state of mind?

passages.paragraph: The narrator considers travelling back home by bus and train through Patna and Benaras. However, he feels tired and homesick. Therefore he decides to buy a plane ticket from the Nepal Airlines office for the next day.
questions: What route does the narrator first consider for returning home?
Through which cities does he plan to travel?
Why does he change his plan?
What decision does he finally make?
From where does he buy the ticket?

passages.paragraph: Before leaving Kathmandu, the narrator notices a flute seller standing quietly in the square. The man carries several bamboo flutes tied to a pole. From time to time he plays a soft tune that rises above the noise of the market.
questions: Whom does the narrator notice in the square?
What does the flute seller carry?
What are the flutes made of?
How does the flute music sound?
What effect does the music have in the noisy market?
